title: $lowercase
text: $lowercase Convert a string to lowercase. $lowercase ( String { , "NlsLocale" | "classic" } ) Example: vConverted = $lowercase ("The STRING", "nlslocale") Parameters Table 1. Parameters Parameter Data Type Description String String String to convert to lowercase; can be a string, field (or indirect reference to a field), variable, or function that evaluates to a string. NlsLocale String Apply locale-based rules based on the value of the $nlslocale , as specified by $nlslocale or $NLS_LOCALE . classic String Ignore locale when converting data; apply one-to-one character conversion according to Unicode definitions. Return Values String converted to lowercase. Use Allowed in all component types. Description The way in which strings are converted depends on the NLS settings in effect. If the second argument is omitted, the value of $nlscase is used to determine case conversion behavior. For more information, see Case Conversion . For example, the following ProcScript converts the Turkish word KIPIRTI to lowercase as kipirti . FIELD1 = $lowercase ("KIPIRTI", "classic") ;Result: FIELD1 = kipirti Copy However, Turkish has both a dotted i and an undotted ı , so this case conversion is incorrect. To get the correct conversion, you need to set the locale to Turkish, so that locale-based case conversion is applied. $nlslocale = "tr_TR" ; set locale to Turkish(Turkey) FIELD2 = $lowercase ("KIPIRTI") Result: FIELD2 = kıpırtı Copy Related Links Case Conversion You can use ProcScript statements and functions to convert string from uppercase to lowercase or from lowercase to uppercase. The default conversion behavior is determined by the values of NLS assignment settings and ProcScript functions, which control locale-based processing. lowercase (deprecated) Convert a string to lowercase. $uppercase Convert the specified string uppercase. uppercase (deprecated) Convert the specified string to uppercase.
